level_signals: 4-6+ years of experience as a technical program manager or in product/engineering operations
Direct ownership of complex enterprise deployments or large-scale internal cross-functional initiatives
Operates without direct authority over executing teams

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4-6+ years of experience as a technical program manager or in product/engineering operations, with direct ownership of either complex enterprise deployments or large-scale internal cross-functional initiatives

Track record running high-stakes customer implementations or feature builds at the design-partner or strategic-deal tier, where scope is ambiguous, requirements evolve mid-build, and the customer's success is tied directly to yours

Demonstrated ability to drive programs without authority — an outcome-oriented but collaborative approach that unites teams around shared milestones and holds the work accountable end-to-end with influence
Structured, rigorous approach to ambiguity — able to define program scope, sequence dependencies, surface blockers early, and keep multiple concurrent workstreams moving without losing resolution

Enough technical fluency to engage credibly with engineering teams — able to ask the right questions, pressure-test tradeoffs, and translate between technical and business contexts without going deep on implementation
Exceptional written and verbal communicator — able to calibrate depth, format, and framing for customers, engineering leads, executive sponsors, and external partners alike

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Hands-on experience with LLMs or conversational AI — you've shipped something with these systems, whether in a professional or personal context
A track record of moving programs through legal, security, engineering, and customer approval gates — able to anticipate friction points and keep timelines intact

scope.surface: End-to-end delivery of complex enterprise customer implementations (design partnerships, strategic deals, enterprise go-lives) and internal cross-functional programs enabling enterprise readiness
scope.business_model: B2B enterprise SaaS — conversational AI platform sold to large enterprises
primary_metrics_inferred: Operational scalability as customer base and deal complexity grow
core_responsibilities: Partner with a forward-deployed working team to drive operational excellence and program management for complex customer implementations end-to-end
Translate customer requirements and feedback into structured program plans
Drive internal cross-functional programs — model lifecycle management, security and compliance buildouts, external certifications — from kickoff through completion

green_flags: Clear description of the team (APM) and how TPM fits within it
Well-defined core responsibilities with concrete examples (design partnerships, model lifecycle management, security certifications)
Backed by top-tier investors (a16z, Accel, Bain Capital Ventures, Coatue, Index Ventures) — signals financial stability
Named enterprise customers (Avis, Cash App, Chime, Oura) provide credibility to the platform's scale
Encourages candidates to apply even without every nice-to-have
red_flags: No specific city or office location mentioned despite being explicitly an in-office company
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed
Equity not mentioned — unusual for a VC-backed startup at this stage

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years range and scope of responsibilities)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and customer examples)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.cities_listed (no specific city named for this role despite in-office requirement)
compensation.equity_mentioned (inferred as false; equity not referenced anywhere in JD)
ambiguities: The JD states 'We're an in-office company' but does not name the city or office location for this role. Location type is set to 'onsite' but cities_listed is empty.
Years range '4-6+' is stated as a range with an open upper bound; minimum is set to 4 and maximum left null to reflect the open-ended nature.
